text.title: Linear Workflow: Triage, GitHub Integration, Views
text.summary: Triage process, GitHub sync configuration, custom view patterns, and keyboard shortcut usage.

rule.statement: Process triage in this order: valid? → urgent? → scoped? → ready for backlog. Invalid issues go to Closed/Duplicate immediately; unscoped issues get "Needs Refinement" label and stay in triage.
rule.rationale: Triage order prevents time spent scoping issues that should be closed; "Needs Refinement" creates a visible queue for refinement sessions without cluttering the backlog.

rule.statement: Triage is a human-only step; agents must not auto-close or auto-route issues from the triage inbox without a human checkpoint.
rule.rationale: Triage decisions require context that agents cannot infer from issue text alone (duplicate detection, organizational priority, business context).

rule.statement: Configure GitHub integration: branch format "{username}/bak-{issue_number}-{issue_title}", PR-opened → "In Review", PR-merged → "Done", commit pattern "Fixes BAK-\\d+".
rule.rationale: Consistent branch naming enables bidirectional traceability; fix commit pattern auto-links PRs to issues; auto-transitions eliminate manual status updates.

rule.statement: Store issue identifiers (e.g., BAK-123) in git branch names and PR titles to enable bidirectional traceability between Linear issues and GitHub PRs.
rule.rationale: Linear's GitHub integration matches PRs to issues by identifier in branch name or PR title; without this, no automatic status transitions occur.

rule.statement: Create four standard views: my_work (assignee:me, group by priority), team_board (team filter, group by status, show estimates), bugs_triage (label:bug, status:triage, sort by created date), release_scope (project filter, status:-canceled).
rule.rationale: These four views cover the four daily PM workflows: individual task focus, team coordination, bug queue, and release tracking — without proliferating context-specific views.

rule.statement: Use issueSearch with server-side filters rather than fetching issues and filtering client-side — server-side filtering is 10x faster on large workspaces.
rule.rationale: Client-side filtering on 5000+ issues requires fetching all pages then discarding most results; server-side filters push this work to Linear's query engine.
